desc: Naxos is the Cyclades island for travellers who want real beach time without giving up village depth, food identity and a sense of space. Portara sunsets, long west-coast sands, marble inland settlements and farm-rooted cooking make it feel more rounded and less scene-driven than some of its better-known neighbours.

todo.name: Portara
todo.desc: Time the marble gateway for sunset, when the harbour and old town are at their most photogenic.

todo.name: Agios Prokopios and Plaka beaches
todo.desc: Use the west-coast beaches for the island's easiest sand-and-swim days.

todo.name: Halki and Apeiranthos villages
todo.desc: Head inland for marble lanes, distilleries and a different Cycladic rhythm.

todo.name: Temple of Demeter
todo.desc: Pair the inland temple with village stops to break up a beach-heavy itinerary.

todo.name: Halki kitron stop
todo.desc: Use Halki for a slower inland pause with distillery culture and a different rhythm from the coast.

todo.name: Early bus or drive into the interior
todo.desc: Make time for one mountain-village loop so Naxos feels like more than beaches stitched together by the port.

wifi.note: Usually reliable in Chora and the main west-coast beach belt, but inland villages and smaller family-run stays are where patchier setups show up, so mobile backup still helps.

prac.fltNote: Naxos is usually reached via Athens: easyJet currently sells London Gatwick-Athens nonstop, then you connect onward by short domestic flight or a Piraeus ferry. The real bottleneck is the island leg rather than the UK leg, because summer ferry capacity, Meltemi disruption and small-aircraft seats all tighten together.
prac.lang: Greek; English is common in tourism areas
prac.visa: Schengen rules apply; UK/Irish travellers should verify 90-in-180-day limits and passport validity before travel.
alerts: Ferry schedules are weather-sensitive in windy Meltemi periods.
Car and scooter rentals sell out in peak weeks.
Small-airport seats via Athens are limited and book early.

sweet: June and September are usually Naxos at its best: beach weather is strong, the island feels fully alive, and you avoid some of the July-August crush on ferries, rentals and waterfront rooms.
